title: ITSI Service Modeling for Catalyst Center
dataSources: `index=catalyst sourcetype=cisco:dnac:*` (all Catalyst Center sourcetypes via ITSI KPI base searches). Requires Splunk IT Service Intelligence (ITSI) installed and licensed. KPI base searches reference the same Catalyst Center data but are consumed through ITSI service models, glass tables, and episode analytics rather than standalone dashboards.
app: `Cisco Catalyst Add-on for Splunk` (Splunkbase 7538) + `Splunk IT Service Intelligence`
spl: | from datamodel:"ITSI_KPI_Summary" | where service_name="*Catalyst Center*" | stats latest(kpi_urgency) as urgency latest(alert_level) as alert_level by service_name, kpiid, itsi_kpi_id | sort -urgency
description: Creates an ITSI service model for Catalyst Center infrastructure with KPIs derived from device health, client health, network health, and issue data, enabling service-centric monitoring and correlation.
value: ITSI service modeling transforms per-device/per-sourcetype monitoring into business-service health, enabling correlation across all Catalyst Center data streams and integration with IT service management.
implementation:
Create an ITSI service for Catalyst Center with the following KPI base searches:

1. **Device Health KPI:** `index=catalyst sourcetype="cisco:dnac:devicehealth" | stats avg(overallHealth) as device_health count(eval(overallHealth<50)) as unhealthy_devices`
2. **Client Health KPI:** `index=catalyst sourcetype="cisco:dnac:clienthealth" | stats avg(scoreDetail{}.scoreCategory.value) as client_health`
3. **Network Health KPI:** `index=catalyst sourcetype="cisco:dnac:networkhealth" | stats latest(healthScore) as network_health`
4. **Issue Volume KPI:** `index=catalyst sourcetype="cisco:dnac:issue" (priority="P1" OR priority="P2") status!="RESOLVED" | stats count as critical_issues`
5. **Compliance KPI:** `index=catalyst sourcetype="cisco:dnac:compliance" complianceStatus="NON_COMPLIANT" | stats count as non_compliant_devices`

Configure entity types: Network Device (matched on `deviceName`), Site (matched on `siteId`).

Set thresholds: Green (device_health>75, critical_issues=0), Yellow (device_health 50-75 OR critical_issues 1-3), Red (device_health<50 OR critical_issues>3).
visualization: ITSI Service Analyzer deep dive; glass table with KPI tiles; Episode Review for correlated episodes; optional deep link to Splunk dashboards for raw SPL drilldown.
knownFalsePositives:
**ITSI KPI flickering at a threshold boundary triggering frequent episodes.** When a KPI value oscillates around a threshold boundary (e.g., CPU at 79-81% with an 80% threshold), ITSI repeatedly opens and closes episodes. This creates noise without indicating a real problem. Distinguish by checking whether the KPI value is within 5% of the threshold and whether episodes are opening and closing within minutes. Suppress by configuring KPI threshold hysteresis (e.g., alert at 80%, clear at 75%) or using adaptive thresholds.

**Catalyst Center API poll timing creating artificial KPI gaps.** If the TA polls at 900-second intervals but the ITSI KPI is evaluated at 300-second intervals, there will be gaps where no data is available, potentially showing the KPI as unknown or N/A. Distinguish by checking whether KPI gaps align with the TA poll schedule. Suppress by aligning the KPI calculation window with the TA poll interval or using `| fillnull` to carry forward the last known value.

**Service dependency tree amplifying a single KPI failure to multiple service alerts.** In a hierarchical service model, a single failing KPI in a child service can propagate to parent services, generating alerts at every level. Distinguish by identifying the root cause KPI — the lowest-level service with a critical KPI is the true source. Suppress by configuring ITSI to identify root cause services and alert only on the root cause, not on every affected parent.

**Entity discovery adding new devices that lack historical KPI baselines.** When Catalyst Center discovers new devices, the corresponding ITSI entities have no historical data for baseline comparison. KPIs may show anomalous values until sufficient history is collected. Distinguish by checking whether the entity was recently discovered (entity creation time within the last 7 days). Suppress by applying a 7-day burn-in period for new entities before including them in adaptive threshold calculations.

references.title: Catalyst Center Intent API Reference — Cisco DevNet

detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Splunk IT Service Intelligence (ITSI) installed and licensed on the search head cluster.
• Cisco Catalyst Add-on for Splunk (Splunkbase 7538) operational with all relevant inputs enabled to `index=catalyst`.
• ITSI entity discovery configured for Catalyst Center devices (or manual entity import from device inventory).
• At least 7 days of Catalyst Center health data for ITSI baseline establishment.
• RBAC: ITSI admin role for service and KPI configuration.

Step 1 — Configure data collection
ITSI service modeling uses the existing Catalyst Center data — no additional collection is needed. The ITSI integration layer consists of:
1. **Entities:** Catalyst Center-managed devices, imported from `cisco:dnac:device` or `cisco:dnac:devicehealth` data.
2. **Services:** Logical groupings (e.g., "Campus Network Health", "Wireless Infrastructure", "WAN Edge") with KPIs derived from Catalyst Center sourcetypes.
3. **KPIs:** Key performance indicators computed from Splunk searches against `index=catalyst`.

Entity import SPL:
```spl
index=catalyst sourcetype="cisco:dnac:device" | stats latest(managementIpAddress) as ip latest(deviceType) as type latest(siteId) as site by deviceName | rename deviceName as host
```

Step 2 — Service and KPI configuration

Define a service hierarchy in ITSI:
• **Parent service:** "Catalyst Center Network" — aggregates all child services.
• **Child services:** "Device Infrastructure Health" (KPI: avg overallHealth from devicehealth), "Client Experience" (KPI: avg healthScore from clienthealth), "Network Health" (KPI: avg overallHealth from networkhealth), "Active Issues" (KPI: count of P1/P2 issues), "Security Posture" (KPI: count of critical advisories).

Sample KPI base search for Device Infrastructure Health:
```spl
index=catalyst sourcetype="cisco:dnac:devicehealth" | stats avg(overallHealth) as avg_health min(overallHealth) as min_health dc(deviceName) as device_count | eval urgency=case(avg_health<25,"critical", avg_health<50,"high", avg_health<75,"medium", 1==1,"normal")
```

Understanding this KPI:
• **`avg(overallHealth)`**: The primary KPI value — average device health across the fleet.
• **`min(overallHealth)`**: Worst-case health — catches individual device failures even when the average is acceptable.
• **`urgency`**: Maps the KPI value to ITSI severity levels using the same thresholds as Catalyst Center Assurance.

Step 3 — Validate
• **Service health:** After creating the service and KPIs, verify that the service shows a valid health score in ITSI > Service Analyzer.
• **Entity count:** Verify that the ITSI entity count matches the device count in Catalyst Center.
• **KPI accuracy:** Compare the ITSI KPI values with the corresponding Catalyst Center Assurance health scores.

Step 4 — Operationalize
• **Glass Tables:** Create ITSI Glass Tables for visual representation of the Catalyst Center service hierarchy with color-coded health indicators.
• **Episode rules:** Configure notable event aggregation policies to group related KPI breaches into episodes (e.g., multiple device health degradations at the same site = one site-level episode).
• **Correlation:** Link Catalyst Center ITSI services with other ITSI services (application performance, user experience) for end-to-end service health visibility.

Step 5 — Troubleshoot
• **KPI shows N/A:** The KPI base search may not be returning data. Verify the SPL runs successfully in the search bar and returns results within the KPI calculation window.
• **Entity discovery not finding devices:** Check the entity import search and verify `index=catalyst sourcetype="cisco:dnac:device"` has data. Entity discovery must be configured with the correct search and field mappings.
• **KPI flickering between thresholds:** Configure hysteresis (alert at 80%, clear at 75%) or increase the KPI calculation window to smooth out transient fluctuations.
• **Service health not propagating to parent:** Verify the service dependency tree is correctly configured with appropriate weight and importance settings.
• **No data for some sourcetypes:** verify each modular input is enabled in the TA; check `index=_internal` for API error or endpoint timeout messages per input.
• **NULL or missing values across multiple inputs:** a credential or connectivity issue affects all inputs; check the base URL and authentication token first.
• **Fewer events than expected:** check the service account role permissions; credential access denied errors indicate the account lacks required RBAC roles.
grandmaExplanation: We create a service-level view of your Catalyst Center infrastructure in ITSI, combining all the health data into a single service health score that shows how the network is serving the business.
